competitors.label: HTML images
competitors.selectionReason: This MDN guide is the closest canonical page for alt text, image fallback behavior, and the accessibility role of the img element.
theirStrengths: MDN is strong on core image markup, what alt is for, and how images behave when they fail to load.
theirGaps: The page is less direct about the production decision between decorative, informative, and functional images.

realWorldUseCases: The real production decision is whether an image is decorative, informative, or functional
followUpConfusion: If a logo links back home, alt like "FrontendAtlas home" is usually more helpful than "Company logo".

realWorldUseCases: MDN explains image fallback and alternative text clearly, but it is less centered on the production choice between decorative, informative, and functional images.
actionableExamples: It still has direct image-markup examples, so the actionable-example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our answer is now more explicit about linked logos, icon-only actions, and when alt should describe the destination or action instead of the picture.

actionableExamples: Keep the three-way decision table visible and add a complex-image follow-up only if future reviews still need it.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical image reference, but our page now does a better job of turning alt text into a practical decision: decorative, informative, or functional, with the linked-logo follow-up people usually miss.

competitors.label: HTML Interview Questions and Answers
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's HTML interview bank is directly relevant because accessibility, semantics, and image-related questions are part of core HTML interview coverage.
theirStrengths: It is a strong interview-prep hub with broad HTML fundamentals coverage.
theirGaps: The category page is not dedicated to the exact alt-text decision path or the functional-image edge cases.

realWorldUseCases: The real production decision is whether an image is decorative, informative, or functional
followUpConfusion: If nearby text already says the same thing, keep the alt short and avoid repeating the visible label or stuffing extra SEO keywords.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd is clearly relevant for HTML interviews, but the bank page is less anchored to the specific alt-text review decision that teams trip over in production.
actionableExamples: It remains a competitive interview-prep source, so the example bar stays close.
followUpConfusion: Our answer now handles the likely follow-up confusion more directly: decorative versus functional images, linked logos, and redundant nearby text.

actionableExamples: Keep the functional-image example and three-way decision block near the opening so the page stays skimmable.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is broadly useful for HTML prep, but our page is more specific about the concrete alt-text decisions and follow-up mistakes that make an answer operationally useful.

competitors.label: Images
competitors.selectionReason: web.dev has a topic-direct accessibility guide for image purpose, context, decorative versus informative images, and alternative text best practices.
theirStrengths: It is very strong on the image-purpose decision path, including decorative, informative, functional, and complex images.
theirGaps: It is broader and less compressed as a trivia-style interview answer.

realWorldUseCases: The real production decision is whether an image is decorative, informative, or functional
followUpConfusion: If an icon-only button opens search, the alt or accessible name should expose the action, not the artwork.

realWorldUseCases: web.dev is strong on image-purpose framing, but our page now turns that framing into a tighter production decision that is easier to apply quickly.
actionableExamples: web.dev still has stronger breadth, especially across more image categories, so the example criterion stays tied rather than flipping fully to us.
followUpConfusion: Our answer now gives clearer short follow-ups for linked logos, icon buttons, redundant nearby text, and SEO stuffing.

actionableExamples: If this page needs another pass later, add one compact complex-image note without turning the trivia answer into a long guide.

competitors.winReason: web.dev is broader, but our page is now stronger as a compact interview answer because it converts the alt-text topic into a direct production decision and answers the common functional-image follow-ups cleanly.
